storyline:
GEO Group (GEO) + MicroStrategy (MSTR) — the 2024-election concurrency test. Trump's Nov-5 win was one catalyst with two theses: an immigration crackdown (GEO, the largest private ICE-detention operator) and a crypto-friendly agenda (MSTR, the leveraged Bitcoin proxy). This book asks whether the solution can ride two gems at once. The lesson: GEO was a clean policy event, but MSTR's momentum thesis never resolved — no ceasefire, no reopened chokepoint — so a weak curator holds it through a ~45% crash (the failure mode the gate exists to prevent). The weekly cadence also can't out-run insiders on election plays. What we'd want: hold GEO and exit MSTR as the crypto rally peaks. Sonnet5 is the only curator that managed it (+20%); the others bled to ~−20%.
This gem’s event-agent (ev2) — catalyst: Trump win drives mass-deportation ICE detention expansion. Exit: still live — none - no ICE contract awards yet, deportation policy intent unreversed, arc still ahead.
Co-discovered events the curator juggled (not this dashboard's gem):
  • defensive · GLD — catalyst: always-on defensive (GLD) floor agent. Exit: still live (catalyst active).
  • ev1 · CEG — catalyst: Constellation announces Three Mile Island restart deal with Microsoft. Exit: still live — NRC license amendment for Crane restart still unapproved; no reversal of AI power-demand driver.

seeds.title: Trump's win sets a mass-deportation executive-order buildout — GEO Group is the lead ICE-detention contractor
seeds.snippet: Trump's election win makes a mass-deportation executive-order push the federal mandate, expanding ICE detention capacity - a specific, datable policy action. GEO Group (GEO), the largest private-detention operator, holds the federal detention contracts and is the direct beneficiary of the buildout. Still a niche, under-owned pure-play on the policy, not the megacaps.

CEG.thesis: Constellation announces Three Mile Island restart deal with Microsoft
CEG.exit_case: none — deal just announced; NRC approval and 2027 restart still pending
CEG.assessment: Constellation-Microsoft PPA for Three Mile Island (Crane Clean Energy Center) restart is a first-of-its-kind AI-power-demand deal. Stock and nuclear peers (NuScale, Oklo, Nano Nuclear) popped on the news, but the actual mechanics — NRC approval, plant recommissioning, 2027 target restart — are all still ahead, keeping this from being fully resolved/priced. CEG is the clean pure-play vehicle as the direct counterparty and asset owner.
CEG.exit_advice: Hold. Deal announced this week but restart requires NRC license renewal and targets 2027 online date — concrete regulatory/execution catalysts remain ahead. Not yet priced as a resolved binary.

CEG.thesis: Constellation announces Three Mile Island restart deal with Microsoft
CEG.exit_case: none
CEG.assessment: No news this week specific to the deal (unrelated Ternium sustainability report items only). Thesis remains intact: Crane Clean Energy Center restart requires NRC approval and recommissioning work still pending, with 2027 target restart far from resolved. No reversal of driving condition (AI datacenter power demand driving nuclear restarts) and no evidence of mainstream saturation replacing the specific regulatory/operational catalysts still ahead. Continue holding CEG as the clean pure-play counterparty/asset owner.
CEG.exit_advice: Hold CEG; NRC approval/relicensing process and 2027 restart timeline remain ahead, deal still not fully priced through regulatory milestones.

Continue holding CEG as the clean pure-play counterparty/owner of the asset.

CEG.thesis: Constellation announces Three Mile Island restart deal with Microsoft
CEG.exit_case: NRC license amendment still pending; restart approval not yet granted—catalyst unresolved
CEG.assessment: Six weeks post-announcement, coverage has broadened into generic 'nuclear stocks for AI' listicles and performance-recap pieces (30% gain since deal), signaling rising mainstream attention but not full saturation displacing the actual regulatory catalyst. The concrete pending step—NRC approval of the license amendment for Crane Clean Energy Center restart—has not occurred; no news this week confirms any decision. Driving condition (AI datacenter demand for nuclear) remains intact and if anything is broadening across peers (Oklo, NuScale, SMR), which is not a reversal. This is a multi-year regulatory arc; the real catalyst (NRC decision, recommissioning milestones, 2027 target) is still scheduled/expected ahead, not speculative. Continue holding CEG as the clean pure-play counterparty and asset owner until the regulatory pathway resolves or definitively stalls.
CEG.exit_advice: Hold CEG; exit only once NRC license amendment is granted/denied or recommissioning timeline is confirmed/derailed, or if AI power-demand narrative reverses.

CEG.thesis: Constellation announces Three Mile Island restart deal with Microsoft
CEG.exit_case: FERC rejected similar Amazon-Talen nuclear PPA, raising regulatory risk though not this specific deal.
CEG.assessment: Six weeks post-signing, the specific NRC license amendment catalyst remains unresolved. This week's news is notable not for resolving the Constellation deal but for introducing regulatory headline risk via a different but analogous deal's rejection (Amazon-Talen). This could either delay/complicate CEG's own NRC pathway or be idiosyncratic to Talen's structure — too early to tell. Strong underlying earnings (30% EPS growth) support the fundamental thesis of nuclear-for-AI demand. No mainstream saturation has fully displaced the regulatory catalyst as the key event still ahead; coverage remains mixed between earnings strength and regulatory pushback on the broader theme.
CEG.exit_advice: Hold CEG; NRC license amendment for Crane Clean Energy Center restart still pending. The FERC rejection concerns Amazon/Talen's separate interconnection deal structure, not Constellation's PPA — but it signals regulators are scrutinizing datacenter-nuclear co-location arrangements, a risk to watch closely for read-through to CEG's own NRC approval path. Strong Q3 EPS growth (30%) validates the earnings trajectory but the concrete regulatory catalyst (NRC decision on Crane restart) has not landed. Driving condition (AI power demand for nuclear) remains intact; no reversal. Continue holding CEG as pure-play counterparty/asset owner.

GEO.thesis: Trump win drives mass-deportation ICE detention expansion
GEO.exit_case: none — Trump not yet inaugurated, no executive order signed, catalyst entirely pending
GEO.assessment: Trump's election win is the discrete catalyst; mass-deportation policy requires ICE detention capacity buildout, and GEO Group is the named largest private-prison/detention contractor positioned to benefit. This is day one post-election — policy implementation (EOs, contract awards, capacity expansion) is still entirely ahead and unpriced beyond initial election-reaction pop. Under-the-radar relative to eventual scale if EOs materialize as expected.
GEO.exit_advice: Hold GEO through inauguration and first executive orders on deportation/detention; watch for signed EOs, new ICE detention contracts, or capacity expansion announcements as confirmation. Exit only if administration walks back mass-deportation plans or curbs detention expansion.

Inauguration and initial EO barrage have passed (precondition met), but the actual policy mechanics - detention contract awards, capacity expansion announcements, funding appropriations - remain entirely ahead and unannounced. This extended silence post-inauguration is worth flagging but does not meet exit criteria: no reversal of driving condition (deportation policy is being implemented, not reversed), no resolution of the specific catalyst (no contracts awarded), and no mainstream saturation (coverage remains generic macro with zero GEO-specific tie-in). The arc is incomplete - concrete contract/capacity news is still a reasonably expected near-term event given active policy implementation, not mere speculation. Continued patience warranted.
lifecycle.exit_advice: Hold GEO; the specific catalyst (ICE/BOP detention contract awards, capacity expansion funding) has not occurred. Deportation policy intent is confirmed via EOs but the actual contract/capacity mechanics that move GEO specifically remain unannounced and unpriced. Thirteenth straight week of silence on GEO-specific news is unusual but not a driving-condition reversal - deportation enforcement is being actively pursued, not curtailed. No mainstream saturation tying GEO to this theme exists yet, so the under-the-radar edge is intact. Continue holding through this silence; watch for first concrete ICE/BOP detention contract news as the real trigger.

Inauguration and initial EO barrage occurred weeks ago (precondition met), but the actual policy mechanics that constitute the specific catalyst - detention contract awards, capacity expansion, funding appropriations - remain unannounced. This is an unusually long lag between precondition and catalyst materialization, which raises some concern about timing, but nothing has reversed the driving condition (deportation policy is actively being pursued via EOs and enforcement actions) nor resolved the specific catalyst (no contracts signed). Coverage remains completely generic macro with no mainstream connection to GEO, meaning the edge - if the catalyst does materialize - remains unpriced and under-the-radar. Continued patience is warranted since concrete contract news is a plausible near-term event given active policy implementation, not mere speculation, though the extended silence is a genuine risk factor worth monitoring closely.

No reversal of policy intent, no resolution, still under-the-radar on GEO-specific tie-in despite 14 weeks of silence post-election and 4 weeks post-inauguration.

This week's flow is pure generic macro (equities mixed, oil dropping on Trump/geopolitical uncertainty) with no policy specifics. Precondition (inauguration, EOs) met a month ago; the actual catalyst - detention contract awards, capacity expansion, funding appropriations - remains unannounced. Extended lag is a genuine risk factor but does not constitute reversal or resolution. Deportation enforcement continues to ramp per broader news context outside this feed, keeping driving condition intact. GEO remains cleanest pure-play private detention vehicle; no mainstream saturation has occurred tying the stock to this specific theme, so if/when contract news lands, it should still be a fresh catalyst rather than priced-in.
lifecycle.exit_advice: Hold GEO. No reversal of deportation policy intent, no resolution of specific catalyst (still no detention contract awards/capacity expansion announcements), and coverage remains completely generic macro with zero GEO-specific tie-in - genuinely still under-the-radar. Fifteenth week of silence is unusual but not exit-worthy absent reversal or resolution. Watch closely for first concrete contract/appropriations news as the real trigger.

lifecycle.exit_case: none - no ICE contract awards yet, deportation policy intent unreversed, arc still ahead
lifecycle.assessment: Sixteenth consecutive week of zero GEO/ICE/detention-specific news despite inauguration and EO barrage having occurred over a month ago. This week's flow is pure generic Trump macro (tariffs, Ukraine minerals deal, Greenland, bitcoin selloff, recession fears) with no policy specifics tying to detention capacity or GEO contracts. The extended lag between precondition (inauguration) and actual catalyst (contract awards/funding appropriations) is now unusually long and a genuine risk factor, but nothing has reversed the driving condition - mass deportation enforcement remains the administration's stated priority and is being implemented via EOs/ICE operations reported elsewhere. No resolution has occurred (no detention contracts signed or capacity expansion formally announced), and no mainstream saturation has connected GEO specifically to this theme - coverage remains entirely generic macro. The arc is incomplete: concrete contract/appropriation news is still a plausible near-term event tied to active policy implementation, not mere speculation, so the position remains genuinely under-the-radar. Continued patience warranted, though the silence duration warrants closer monitoring going forward.
lifecycle.exit_advice: Hold GEO; continue monitoring for detention contract awards/capacity expansion announcements as the real catalyst - inauguration/EOs were only the precondition, not the resolution.
